---
title: "Creating The Documents Folder"
---

Before any documents can make it into the CIC repository from Guidewire, a special folder needs to be added to the repository to ensure documents are stored correctly.

The CIC environment needs to be fully provisioned and the hfgw-cc-content-model project needs to be deployed.
To create the documents folder:

1.  Log in to the Studio Admin application.
2.  Navigate to DevOps > Application Instances. See the Hyland Automate Administration documentation for general information on using the Admin application. The Application Instances page is displayed.
3.  Find the hfgw-cc-content-model project and click the workspace link for that project. The Workspace application is displayed.
4.  Click the drop-down arrow next to the New File Upload button and click Create Folder. The Create New Folder dialog box is displayed.
5.  Enter hfgw-cc-documents in the Folder Name field.
6.  Enter SysFolder in the Folder Category field.
7.  Click Create Folder. The new folder is added to the repository.
8.  Click the check box next to the newly created hfgw-cc-content-model folder.
9.  Click the vertical ellipsis button in the top bar and then click Permissions.   ![Screenshot of the vertical ellipsis button highlighted](data:image/gif;base64,)   The Manage access permissions pane is displayed.
10.  In the User Group Access Levels section, set the Guidewire Content Users user group to Read and Write.
11.  In the User Group Access Levels section, set the Guidewire Solution Administrators user group to Everything.
12.  Click Save.
